reviews.review_msg: Took this course in Fall 2022. I actually enjoyed Bob Ley's lectures. Study from the textbook cause those are going to be exactly on the tests. Though the written questions, they are straight from his lectures. I found myself interested in his lectures for the full 3 hours and that was a 9:30 am course. Visit his office hours and meet the guy.

Do not take this course! Prof is super unattached and does not respond to his emails. The material is interesting although listening to MP3 recordings with no slides is not. He is constantly canceling class and honestly just seems like he is not interested in the course or his students. The textbook was also extremely difficult to find!

The whole class is comprised of 3 exams, and they are extremely difficult. He says the multiple choice is pulled straight from textbook bank, and it shows, because they are truly random and never covered in class. He missed about half the lectures and uploaded MP3s from previous semesters. Can be an interesting lecturer with real life examples.

No lecture slides, so it makes the course a little difficult to follow and understand the more important concepts. The tests are extremely specific, so get ready to memorize every little detail of the textbook! Bob is not very engaging, which is a shame because the content is interesting, but the way it is taught did not make me want to learn.

It's a shame...I was really interested in the course content but this prof but is extremely detached from his students and does not care at all. His exams are extremely difficult and unfair. You will be tested on specific details that will eventually lower your motivation and disconnect you from the course and make you regret staying in this class.

Many of us had trouble getting ahold of the textbook in the first place which is concerning considering that the exams were 90% based on the textbook. Monotone lectures with no slides, not to mention he would often upload lecture recordings days late. Unfortunate that he is the only prof that teaches this course, material was actually interesting.

Heavy course loud, topics were interesting. It's hard because you have to manage your time well, and stay on top of things. Only sound recordings, no PowerPoint or notes. Just him talking for 2.5 hours. I don't think he knows some people learn better with visual aids, I had to rely heavily on the textbook. TA's were the only form of communication

This guy sucks. He clearly has no interest in making his course enjoyable or interesting. As far as the online world goes, prepare to get screwed by a technologically inept professor. Expect unimaginative exam questions that take a minute to read while being offered about 50 seconds per question. STAY AWAY if possible.

Does he pride himself on low grade averages? perhaps. Make sure to memorize the textbook because the exams are more than half fill in the blank pulled right out of it. The only effort he seems to show is ensuring his audio-only lectures are extremely dull and extremely important. I have never felt less engaged in a class and it is showing.

One would assume that a professor of personality would have more of it himself, but this is the dullest course ever. Maybe it is the online world that is killing his vibe but I thought it must be a joke when he claims to be doing everything he can to make it interesting. No power points, no pictures, no videos, just 150 minutes of audio lecture.

Nora is such a sweetheart. Fall 2017 was her first year teaching and not as a TA as she mentioned in the last class. However, the slides were lengthy and full and she often read completely off the slides. 2 exams and 1 optional paper to boost your midterm mark if needed. If u go over the slides and understand the material u will do great!

She's very nice and really tries to connect with students, but she lacks teaching experiences. Pros: paper is optional, no project or assignments, dont need txtbk Cons: Iclicker questions at start of every 8:30 class, midterm and final are hard (cumulative too), midterm 40% final 50% so if u fail final.. gg I wouldnt recommend taking this :(

Nora has a lot of potential - she's got great enthusiasm and you can tell she wants to connect with the class, but she's not there yet. She reads information directly off the slides, but since participation is 10% (iClicker questions aren't very engaging, either) you have to sit through the lectures which you could otherwise just read online.

I thought Nora was a really caring teacher, but I just personally didn't like her teaching style. It was good that she wanted to be extra clear, but a lot of times I felt like she spoke to the class like we weren't very smart or were a lot younger. Overall good though, and it was an easy course. Show up and you will do just fine

2 exams are 90%. 10% iclicker at 8:30am. For how exam heavy this course is (relatively few questions on each too, all MC) it is surprising how little direction was given (no mock exam). Required to read textbook, but unclear as to what to focus on ->Too much memorization. I'm disappointed, but hopefully she'll get better with more experience. :/

Had her for personality psyc. She is amazing! Explains everything very well, and her slides give all the information that you need. Exams are super easy too as long as you go through slides. She's very compassionate as well and seems to care about her students success. She even boosted our class marks for our exam by 3 points.

prof is approachable and friendly, but the structure of the class and the assignments were quite confusing at times and disorganized. syllabus was not up to date nor the due dates of exams/assignments, leading to last-minute changes that were unfair for many students. would take again if it was more organized in the future

This is probably one of the easier psyc courses I have taken. The grading criteria for the papers are bit unclear, but other than that the course is easy to do well in if you take good notes. He is very approachable and accommodating. And you learn a lot about yourself in the course with the personality tests you take in the class.

Course is SO FUN! Weekly personality tests don't feel like work because they're interesting ways to learn about yourself. Syllabus seems intimidating, but some assignments are optional if you do well on the others. Lecture videos are posted so most work is done outside of class, but live sessions help consolidate the material. Strongly recommend!

This class has a ridiculous amount of work involved. There are essentially five papers, including three article reflections. While having these, weekly personality tests and discussions due, this class takes so much time outside of the lecture components. It is basically a writing heavy, stats class. Do not recommend. He's kind, but very demanding.

This is probably the worst course Ive ever taken at SFU. The tests are based almost completely on the textbook and the professor makes it a habit to skip lectures. Even then, chapters that are skipped are tested on. Also the assessment criteria is ridiculous considering all this: 50% weightage is just the last exam! And the first two are 25% each.

Lectures are interesting at times but practically useless for knowledge and exams. Practically self taught as lectures barely covered each theory. The exams are hard with very specific questions and harsh marking for short answer responses. Prof tends to cancel class and then upload a monotone 3 hour audio file lecture.

AVOID THIS COURSE. Most unorganized, lazy professor and T.A's I've ever had. Not only does he expect you to listen to his monotone 3 hour weekly audio recorded lectures but you're also required to memorize each word of the textbook. Impossible to succeed. Why should anyone pay hundreds of dollars for a course you teach yourself? Absolute joke.

He's honestly a nice guy however he does not provide lecture slides. This is acceptable for an inclass lecture but not so much for an online course. You are left with long voice recordings and textbook readings. His exams are also poorly designed where you are stuck rushing from start to finish. His questions are very detailed so eat the textbook.
